title: Study Korea — Kairos Education | Korea University & Language Consulting
metaDescription: Expert consulting for foreigners who want to study in Korea. Language programs, degree admissions, and GKS scholarship support. 1-on-1 with Ron Kim — Seoul insider.
scarcity: Kairos works with a limited number of families each year.◆A few places remain for the current cycle.

sticky.msg: Your child's one shot deserves the right counselor.The first consultation is free, with no obligation.

hero.h1: You want to study in Korea. Here's your way in.
hero.lead: Kairos is one-on-one consulting for international students applying to Korean language programs, degree programs, and the fully funded Korean Government Scholarship. You work directly with Ron Kim, who knows these universities from the inside and walks you through every step, from choosing the right program to the visa in your passport.

worries.title: Sound familiar?
worries.lead: Most people who come to Kairos for Korea are stuck on one of these. Each one is solvable with the right plan.

items.title: You don't know where to start.
items.desc: Language program, degree, or scholarship: the options are confusing and the rules change by country.

items.title: You're unsure if your Korean is enough.
items.desc: For language programs you need zero Korean. For degrees and scholarships there is a clear target, and a plan to reach it.

items.title: The visa process looks overwhelming.
items.desc: D-4, D-2, proof of funds, embassy steps. With the right preparation it is a checklist, not a maze.

items.title: The GKS scholarship feels out of reach.
items.desc: It is competitive, but it is winnable with the right strategy, which is exactly where Ron adds the most value.

worries.foot: Ron lives in Seoul and knows these programs from the inside. The path exists. He helps you find it and walk it.

why.title: One of the best education decisions you can make.
why.sub: Korea's universities rank among the top 100 in the world, tuition costs a fraction of the US or UK, and Seoul is one of the most exciting cities anywhere to spend your twenties.

cards.title: World-ranked universities.
cards.desc: SNU, KAIST, POSTECH, Korea University, and Yonsei all rank in the global top 100 to 200, and KAIST is top 50 in the world for engineering. These are destination schools, not backups.

cards.title: Far lower cost.
cards.desc: Tuition runs about $3,000 to $8,000 a year, a fraction of US or UK prices. With the fully funded GKS scholarship, studying here can cost you almost nothing.

cards.title: A real international community.
cards.desc: More than 160,000 international students are in Korea right now, and English-taught degrees have grown fast. You will land on a genuinely global campus.

cards.title: Korean opens doors.
cards.desc: Even a year of language study puts Samsung, Hyundai, LG, and HYBE within reach, and a TOPIK certificate is a career asset across Asia.

cards.title: A visa process you can navigate.
cards.desc: The D-4 language visa and D-2 student visa are well structured and achievable with the right preparation. Korean consulates operate in over 100 countries.

cards.title: The timing is on your side.
cards.desc: Korean music, film, food, and fashion are everywhere, and global interest keeps growing. Studying here now puts you ahead of the curve.

proof.title: Seoul-based. One on one.
proof.sub: Specialized in Korea, not everything at once.

stats.label: SNU, Korea U, Yonsei & all majors
With Ron, never a junior agent

proof.note: Visa rules and scholarship terms vary by country and year. Kairos guides preparation but does not control admissions or visa decisions.

pathways.title: Three ways into Korea.
pathways.sub: Whether you are starting from zero Korean or aiming for a full scholarship, there is a clear route. Ron helps you find the one that fits, then walks you through it.

items.name: Language Program
items.best: D-4-1 visa · Study at a Korean language center
items.desc: Three months to two years of immersive Korean, starting from the alphabet. The most accessible way in.
details: Yonsei KLI, Korea U KLC, SNU LEI, Sogang, Ewha
Four intakes a year: March, June, September, December
Roughly $10,000 in proof of funds required
Applied for at your home country's Korean embassy

items.name: Degree Program
items.best: D-2 visa · Undergraduate or graduate degree
items.desc: A full bachelor's, master's, or PhD. Many programs are taught in English with no TOPIK required.
details: SNU, Korea University, Yonsei, KAIST, POSTECH, Ewha

items.name: GKS Scholarship
items.best: Fully funded by the Korean government
items.desc: Full tuition, a monthly stipend of about $900, airfare, and insurance. Highly competitive, where Ron adds the most value.
details: Covers tuition, stipend, airfare, and insurance
Undergrad: 1 year Korean plus a 4-year degree
Grad: 1 year Korean plus a 2 to 3 year MA or PhD
Apply through the embassy or university track

universities.title: Where Kairos can take you.
universities.sub: Ron has the Seoul-insider knowledge to match you with the right institution, whether that is a SKY university, a world-ranked science school, or the best language center for your goals.

universities.name: Korea University
universities.detail: Korean Language Center (KLC) · Business · Law · CS

universities.name: KAIST
universities.detail: Korea Advanced Institute of Science & Technology

universities.name: UNIST
universities.detail: Ulsan National Institute of Science & Tech

universities.footnote: Ron advises on every Korean university and language program. The right fit depends on your goals, budget, major, and language level.

packages.priceNote: Transparent pricing, no inquiry required. Most students start with a free consultation to find the right pathway.

items.q: Do I need to know Korean before a language program?
items.a: No. Language center programs (D-4-1 visa) are built for complete beginners, starting from the alphabet. Yonsei KLI, Korea University KLC, SNU LEI, and Sogang all offer Level 1 courses that assume no prior knowledge. If you have already studied, you will test into a higher level.

items.q: What is the GKS scholarship, and how competitive is it?
items.a: The Global Korea Scholarship (GKS, also called KGSP) is a fully funded government scholarship run by NIIED. It covers tuition, a monthly stipend of about $900, airfare, and health insurance. In 2026 there were roughly 2,000 graduate and 280 undergraduate places worldwide. It is competitive, but with the right strategy, strong candidates from over 100 countries have a real shot. This is exactly what the Summit package is built for.

items.q: Can I apply from any country?
items.a: Korea welcomes students from over 100 countries for both the D-4 language visa and the D-2 student visa, and Korean consulates operate worldwide. Visa processing usually takes one to three weeks depending on where you are. Ron helps you find the right consulate, prepare your documents, and handle any country-specific steps.

items.q: What is TOPIK, and do I need it?
items.a: TOPIK is the official Korean proficiency test, levels 1 to 6. For language center programs you do not need it. For degree programs taught in Korean, most universities want TOPIK Level 3 or 4. For English-taught programs it is not required, though it strengthens your application. GKS does not require TOPIK at the application stage; you complete a year of Korean after acceptance.

items.q: How much does it cost to study in Korea?
items.a: Language programs run about $1,200 to $1,800 per term, plus living costs of roughly $700 to $1,200 a month in Seoul. Degree tuition is about $3,000 to $8,000 a year, well below US or UK equivalents. With the GKS scholarship, tuition and a monthly stipend are fully covered.

items.q: How is Kairos different from other study-abroad agencies?
items.a: Most agencies cover dozens of countries and hand you to junior staff. Kairos is different: every session is with Ron, a Seoul resident with real inside knowledge of these universities, who specializes in Korea and brings the same standards that produced Harvard, Yale, and Columbia admits to your Korea application.

contact.title: Start with a free consultation.
contact.sub: The first consultation is free and there is no obligation. Ron will talk through your goals, your Korean level, your timeline, and whether Kairos is the right fit. No pressure, just an honest conversation with someone who knows Korea from the inside.
contact.location: Seoul, Korea · working with students in 15+ countries
contact.formNote: We reply within 24 hours. Your details stay private and are never shared.

footer.tagline: Boutique college admissions consulting. One student, one counselor, in the US and in Korea.
footer.disc: Kairos Education is an independent consultancy and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any university or institution named on this site. Results vary by student and depend on starting point, timeline, target schools, and individual effort. Past admissions outcomes do not guarantee future results.
